sections.paragraph:
συζεύγνυμι 1 aor. συνέζευξα; pf. pass. ptc. fem. pl. συνεζευγμέναι Ezk 1:11, 23; lit. ‘yoke together’ (X., Cyr. 2, 2, 26), then gener. to make a pair, join together, pair (PGiss 34, 3; Ezk 1:11; Philo), specif. of matrimony (Eur. et al.; cp. X., Oec. 7, 30 νόμος συζευγνὺς ἄνδρα καὶ γυναῖκα; Aristot., HA 7, 6; Jos., Ant. 6, 309; PLond V, 1727, 9) ὸ̔ οὖν ὁ θεὸς συνέζευξεν Mt 19:6; Mk 10:9 (cp. the polytheistic counterpart Nicetas Eugen. 3, 12; 7, 265 Hercher: two lovers οὓς θεὸς [a god] συνῆψε, τίς διασπάσοι;).—M-M. s.v. συνζεύγνυμι.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

fem. fem. = feminine

  1. pl. = plural

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

specif. specif. = specifically

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Nicetas Eugen Nicetas Eugen , XII a.d.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 954
